tiles.question: FLEET ROUTER + PLATO MCP — Both repos shipped
tiles.answer: Two new repos pushed to SuperInstance: (1) fleet-router — FastAPI on :8100, routes queries to cheapest safe model via critical angle table. 4 endpoints, 4 models, 3 providers. Live tested: arithmetic→seed-mini 3.6s $0.01. (2) plato-mcp — PLATO rooms as MCP tools on :8300. 6 tools: list_rooms, read_tiles, write_tile, query_health, route_query, search_tiles. Any MCP-compatible framework can now use PLATO as backend. This is the bridge between Oracle1s repo decomposition and external agent frameworks. Repo→rooms→MCP tools→any client. Both repos: pip install -e . && fleet-router / plato-mcp.

tiles.question: PlatoClaw + Fleet Router = invisible routing
tiles.answer: Merged fleet-router into platoclaw. POST /complete auto-detects domain, routes to cheapest safe model, auto-tiles the result. 5/5 domain detection correct. Live test: 37+48=85 routed to seed-mini in 1.9s. The math is backed in so deep you think about what you are doing with it, not the tools. Install: curl platoclaw/install.sh | bash. Then: platoclaw start, platoclaw complete prompt.

tiles.question: Fleet Stack v1.0 — docker compose up -d
tiles.answer: Full fleet deployment in one command. 6 repos: platoclaw, fleet-router, plato-mcp, fleet-calibrator, fleet-stack, AI-Writings. 21 models calibrated across 3 providers. OpenAI-compatible endpoint. MCP bridge. Dockerfiles for every service. The math is invisible.
